title: VIBRANT VILLAGES PROGRAMME-II (VVP-II)
text:
Press Release:Press Information Bureau

Ministry of Home Affairs

VIBRANT VILLAGES PROGRAMME-II (VVP-II)

Posted On:
18 MAR 2026 2:51PM by PIB Delhi

The Central Government has approved the Vibrant Villages Programme-II (VVP-II), as a Central Sector Scheme, with a total outlay of ₹6839 crores till the Financial Year 2028-29
for comprehensive development  of   select   1954   villages  located   in  blocks  abutting international  land borders (ILBs), other than the Northern Border already covered under VVP-I, in the States/UTs of Arunachal Pradesh, Assam, Bihar, Gujarat, Jammu &Kashmir (UT), Ladakh (UT), Manipur, Meghalaya, Mizoram, Nagaland, Punjab, Rajasthan, Sikkim, Tripura, Uttarakhand, Uttar Pradesh and West Bengal.

The focus areas of interventions for select villages under the programme are (i) livelihood generation, (ii) road connectivity, (iii) energization, (iv) village infrastructure including health facilities, (v) financial inclusion, (vi) empowerment of youth & skill development, (vii) development of cooperatives, SHGs & FPOs for managing livelihood opportunities and maintenance of assets created under the programme, (vii) promotion of tourism, culture and outreach activities, (ix) education infrastructure and (x) television and telecom connectivity.

This was stated by the Minister of State in the Ministry of Home Affairs Shri Nityanand Rai in a written reply to a question in the Rajya Sabha.

*****

RK/RR/PR/PS/SC

(Release ID: 2241684)
